prompt: Classify the image into exactly one of the following categories: Exterior, Interior, Document, or Other. Exterior means any visible outside surface of a vehicle. Interior means seats, dashboard, steering wheel, or cabin view. Document means a photo of paperwork or text with no vehicle visible. Other means anything that does not fit the above. Respond with only the class name.

Determine the primary content of this image and assign exactly one label: Exterior, Interior, Document, or Other. Choose Exterior if the vehicle’s exterior body is visible. Choose Interior if the vehicle’s cabin is visible. Choose Document if the image contains documents or forms without a vehicle. Otherwise choose Other. Return only the label.

You are performing single-label image classification. Select one and only one class: Exterior, Interior, Document, Other. If any part of a vehicle exterior is visible, choose Exterior. If the view is inside the vehicle, choose Interior. If the image is a document without a vehicle, choose Document. If none apply, choose Other. Output the class name only.
